name: Completeness Check
description: Verifies all required elements of a document or analysis are present, ensuring no critical sections or data points are missing.
prompt_template:
<instructions>
Review the following draft to ensure all required elements are included. These typically include:

1. Executive Summary or Introduction
2. Objectives/Scope
3. Methodology/Data Collection
4. Analysis of Findings
5. Recommendations
6. Conclusion
7. References/Bibliography
8. Appendices (if applicable)

For each section, indicate whether it is present and complete ('Yes'/'No'). If 'No', briefly explain what is missing.

Present your findings in a table format:
| Section | Present? | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|

At the end, provide an OVERALL COMPLETENESS SCORE out of 10 and a brief summary of any critical issues that need addressing before final approval.
</instructions>

<draft>
{{draft_content}}
</draft>

variables.name: draft_content
variables.description: The draft text to review for completeness.

beginner.notes: Use this gate as a checklist. It helps ensure no major sections are overlooked, even for simpler documents.
beginner.prompt_modification: Add to the end of your prompt: 'Please also note if any section is incomplete but can be easily filled in with minimal effort.'

intermediate.notes: Use after initial review. Focus on ensuring all critical sections are present and adequately detailed.

advanced.notes: Chain this gate with the Factual Accuracy Gate for comprehensive verification. Consider running it twice — once before, once after revisions — to catch omissions introduced during editing.
advanced.prompt_modification: Add to the end of your prompt: 'Please also evaluate if any additional sections or data points are recommended based on best practices in {{domain}}.'

platform_notes.claude: XML tags are processed natively. Use as-is.
platform_notes.chatgpt: Remove the <instructions> and <draft> XML tags. Paste instructions as plain text followed by the draft.
platform_notes.copilot: Paste instructions as plain text. The <draft> section can be replaced with: 'Here is the draft to review:' followed by the content.

known_limitations: The gate may not catch subtle omissions or sections that are technically present but lack depth.
It relies on the user to specify the required elements accurately. Misalignment between expectations and what's checked can lead to false positives.
